name: Temperate Coniferous Forests
keyCharacteristics: Evergreen conifer-dominated forests with moderate temperatures and high rainfall. Some of the tallest and oldest trees on Earth.
conservationStatus: Old-growth stands significantly reduced by logging. Protected areas preserve some of the most iconic forests. Climate change alters fire regimes and pest dynamics.
description:
Temperate coniferous forests include some of the most impressive forests on Earth, from the towering coast redwoods of California to the ancient temperate rainforests of the Pacific Northwest. They are dominated by evergreen conifers adapted to cooler temperatures.

These forests receive significant precipitation, often exceeding 2000mm annually in coastal regions, much of it as fog drip. The mild, wet conditions support lush undergrowth of ferns, mosses, and lichens.

Found primarily along the western coast of North America, southern Chile, New Zealand, and parts of Europe and East Asia. They include temperate rainforests, which are among the most productive ecosystems on Earth.
